_comment: Machine source of truth for the harness. Human-readable prose lives in the SYNAPSE→H22 checklist HTML; this file is what run.ts executes. Keep the two in sync (that sync IS task 0.3). 'verify' lists the deterministic checks from harness/verify/checks.py that gate the task. 'human_gate' marks a decision the harness must NOT make. 'mode' A runs now on H21; mode B is staged until harness/state/drop.json exists. v2: grafted with SYNAPSE_H22_BOUNDARY.md — D-H22-1/2/4 are now first-class (and critical), and the boundary non-goals are enforced as cross-cutting guardrails.

guardrails._comment: Run on EVERY sprint by checks.py, in addition to the task's own verify list. A guardrail with ok:false ⇒ deterministic FAIL (run.ts short-circuits before the Evaluator and writes a repair ticket). A guardrail with ok:null ⇒ not yet wired ⇒ WARN, never blocks. These enforce SYNAPSE_H22_BOUNDARY.md's non-goals so no Generator round can silently erode the moat: no APEX corpus in scout, no rigging-authoring drift, no mutation that bypasses the ledger, and no newly-introduced phantom hou.* API (dir()-symbol-table-gated).

tasks.title: Split the cp311 vendored seam (sidecar vs abi3)

human_gate.why: Bounded, evidence-backed direction — not a survival cliff. MEASURED: sidecar IPC (harness/notes/gate01_ipc_latency.py) runs p50 ~0.1-0.3ms — ~4 orders under the LLM + ~2s Houdini-cook floor, so IPC does NOT discriminate the arms. cp312/cp313 wheels are pre-cached at harness/state/wheel_cache/ (runbook docs/studio/UPGRADE.md Step 2a), so re-vendor-on-drop is cheap; if H22 ships cp311 this is a no-op. Sidecar is the durable fix, deferrable to the first post-release cycle. Harness verifies the property (brain_answers), not the mechanism; you commit the direction.

tasks.title: Build the H22 probe harness
tasks.note: On H21 the delta vs the H21-pinned constants should be ~empty — that empty diff is the proof the probe works before the drop makes the diff real.

tasks.title: Single-source the version
Fold the duplicate ui/ tree into panel/
Pre-stage the demo scaffold (.hip, shot, OCIO)
Lock the demo runsheet
tasks.note: Content + rehearsal. Harness confirms presence/beat-count; the rehearsal is yours.

tasks.title: Rehearse the clean-machine install on H21
Scaffold the external-MCP provider seam (truth-contract envelope) against a mock APEX MCP
tasks.note: D-H22-1 scaffold. H22's MCP does not exist yet — build + contract-test the provider abstraction against a mock so drop day is wiring-in, not design. The mock's tool list stands in for the real surface; task 1.7 swaps it for the shipped build. Normalize on the Anthropic-shaped envelope, NOT a gateway.

tasks.title: Wire scout to federate the (mock) APEX MCP — consume, don't rebuild
tasks.note: D-H22-2 scaffold. APEX syntax comes ONLY from the federated provider. exists_in_runtime stays authoritative. Scout must own NO APEX-syntax corpus — that is the standing guardrail.

tasks.title: Install H22 clean + Synapse via package

human_gate.why: Can't install software that doesn't exist yet.

tasks.title: Capture the three numbers: Python · USD · PySide

human_gate.why: A human reads these first. Writing drop.json is the Mode-B trigger.

tasks.title: Verify the cp311 fix holds on H22's Python
Fire the probe → API delta report
synapse_doctor green on H22
Panel reads correctly in H22's new theme
Probe the SHIPPED native APEX MCP tool surface; quarantine absent/renamed endpoints (D-H22-4)
tasks.note: Pre-release prose is NOT ground truth. Introspect the real MCP's tool list on the installed H22 before any wiring. Confirmed-absent endpoints auto-quarantine without re-litigation. Gates 2.7/2.8.

tasks.title: Patch the probe deltas (promote probe truth over pinned constants)
COPs — Ocean Spectrum / texture-synth from a prompt
Solaris — scatter / set-dressing from a prompt
Wire both into the command palette (verb × context)
Provenance proof: both land in agent.usd
Karma XPU render the result on H22
Register the shipped APEX MCP as a first-class provider, truth-contract-wrapped, recorded in agent.usd (D-H22-1)
tasks.note: Swap the mock for the real surface verified by 1.7. Every MCP tool call enters the truth contract; the MCP's own validator verdict lands as a provenance INPUT, never as a Synapse claim. This is the H22 critical-path leg.

tasks.title: Scout federates the real APEX MCP; no APEX corpus (D-H22-2)
Dry-run the full runsheet, timed

human_gate.why: Human-timed; harness can't judge dead air.

tasks.title: Record the demo
Demo beat — Synapse orchestrating the native APEX MCP, with receipts
tasks.note: Prove leg. Show the boundary live: Synapse calls the native MCP as one source, the call is truth-contract-wrapped and recorded in agent.usd, and is reversible. The receipts are the one thing the native MCP can't do alone — that's the whole pitch.

tasks.title: Capsule the ship state
Utility flywheel cycle 1 — network-wiring truth (connectivity catalog -> review sweep -> wire_by_label + validator pin)
tasks.note: Cycle contract in spec-U1-wiring-flywheel.md. Anti-runaway anchors: human ratifies new cycle classes (flywheel_queue.json 'ratified'), evidence-linked queue entries only, guardrails every sprint.

tasks.title: Utility flywheel cycle U.5 — LOP/Solaris knowledge (context truth): corpus catalog -> consistency gate -> loader + additive validator advisory phase

human_gate.decision: RATIFIED 2026-07-03 (human sanction) — U.5 accepted as a context-truth cycle class; flywheel_queue.json U.5 now ratified:true

tasks.note: Cycle contract in spec-U5-lop-solaris-flywheel.md. Substantive design call: assignmaterial->material-source ordering is an ADVISORY, not a hard error (reference/sublayer composition arcs author materials too); grid/plane stay hard errors. Anti-runaway: human ratifies the new class (see human_gate).

tasks.title: v6 Session A — scaffold the skeleton: stub every module in the BP00 manifest
tasks.note: Stubs land exactly where BP00's '## Module Manifest' table says; pass bodies; zero hou imports in pure layers. The drop must be COMMITTED first — worktrees fork from HEAD, so an uncommitted docs/v6/ is invisible to this task (docs/v6/INTAKE.md checklist).

tasks.title: v6 Session B — write BP09 (Iteration Controller) at BP00–08 fidelity
tasks.note: propose -> build -> evaluate -> decide -> iterate; convergence/stop logic; max-iteration handling; pulls active strategies from BP07. Spec only — no code.

tasks.title: v6 Session C — spec + BUILD BP10 (Global Knowledge Base): pure Python, zero hou
tasks.note: Recipe store + failure DB + vector schema (spec'd; JSONL is the shipped store) + query API. The one spine piece shippable before H22. The KB public API is pre-frozen by the v6_kb_roundtrip check — BP10 documents it, it does not redesign it.

tasks.title: v6 Session D — pure-Python layers, test-first: gsplat_compare interpretation, scoring rubrics, meta-cognitive stats
tasks.note: Tests FIRST (each blueprint specifies them) so drop day is 'make them pass'.

tasks.title: v6 Miles 1–2 — BP01 Perception + BP02 G-Splat against shipped H22 (highest uncertainty first)
tasks.note: The 1.4 probe report re-ranks V.5–V.7 before work starts; build to survive 'feature X shipped differently'. probe_clean reads THIS worktree's delta artifact — re-fire the probe here first; an honest first-round FAIL until then is expected, not a bug.

tasks.title: v6 Miles 3–4 — BP08 three-tier evaluator (keystone; 01+02 become the engine)
tasks.note: Builds on V.5's output, but worktrees fork from HEAD — merge V.5 before running this, or the engine gets rebuilt divergently in-worktree. run -> merge -> re-run is the intended cadence (docs/v6/PLAN.md POST-DROP).

tasks.title: v6 Miles 5–7 — BP09+BP10 integration: first autonomous cycle, PYRO ONLY
tasks.note: Prove pyro end-to-end before branches (BP07: G-Splat convergence tracks pyro r=0.78, characters r=0.31). Branches 03–07 are production-ordered after this, not corpus-ordered. Needs V.3's KB and V.6's evaluator merged to main first — same run -> merge -> re-run cadence as V.6.

tasks.title: Context track — probe SYNAPSE's create-capability per Houdini context (SOP/LOP/COP/TOP/DOP/MAT), deposit the catalog + review sweep
tasks.note: Read-only vs product code — the probe drives the live handler surface (SynapseHandler.handle), it never edits python/synapse. The catalog is the deliverable; merging it arms C.1–C.6. Re-run per Houdini build.

tasks.title: Context track — SOP: close the top create-gap the catalog surfaces; prove by golden + ratchet
tasks.note: Read the catalog's `gaps` for sop; close the highest-leverage one by extending the HANDLER surface (new/fixed command verbs), never by weakening the probe. The golden ratchet gates: gaps must strictly decrease unless already 0. Re-run the probe in-worktree to refresh the catalog as part of the sprint. The catalog re-ranks C.1–C.6 — a context whose golden already passes with 0 gaps banks trivially, and that is correct.
